summary: Phase 1A Employee Lifecycle tested end-to-end. 19/19 new pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_phase1a_lifecycle.py PASS (profile access control + completeness, documents vault + 2MB cap + no-blob-on-list, onboarding templates seeded w/ 12 tasks, start onboarding computes due_dates & flips employee.status->onboarding, auto-complete on all-tasks-done flips back to active, offboarding 8-clearance default, clearance flips status to in_progress, exit_interview stamps submitted_at, complete blocked while pending, complete flips employee->terminated + user.is_active=false, module gate returns 402 when onboarding module disabled, cross-tenant profile/documents access 403). Frontend smoke verified: HR onboarding list+detail (12 task checkboxes, header, completeness), offboarding list with stat cards (Initiated=1, Relieved=1) and detail (clearance checklist + exit interview form visible), employee directory rows link to /app/employees/:id, profile page shows 10 tabs + completeness bar, HR can edit KYC, employee self-service /app/me correctly gates KYC (pan disabled=true) while allowing Personal (dob disabled=false).
critical_code_review_comments: profile_routes._can_edit_section: super_admin falls through short-circuit only when user.role=='super_admin' — correct — but note the `user.get('company_id') == emp['company_id']` check for other HR roles uses KeyError-prone bracket access on emp['company_id']; employee collection always has company_id so fine, but worth Pydantic hardening.

documents_routes upload: base64 validate=True will reject any whitespace/newlines the client may include. Front-end uses FileReader.readAsDataURL and strips the prefix; fine for now but add lenient decode or document the contract.

onboarding_routes.update_task: when body.status=='done' it stamps completed_* but does NOT clear those fields if status reverts to 'pending'/'in_progress'. Minor — acceptable audit trail but can confuse UI.

offboarding_routes.complete_offboarding: sets relieving_letter_issued/experience_letter_issued/fnf_settled=True on complete even if HR hadn't individually tracked them. Consider making these explicit sub-states.
offboarding_routes.update_clearance: transitions status from initiated->in_progress when ANY item is cleared, but does NOT flip back if all items revert to pending. Minor state-machine gap.
Onboarding/Offboarding endpoints share the single 'onboarding' module gate — offboarding has no separate module entitlement. If product wants to sell offboarding separately, split modules.

employees PATCH endpoint is used by the test teardown to restore employee.status='active' after the terminated-flow test. If that endpoint is later tightened, tests need adjustment — flagged for visibility.

Side-effect of 'complete offboarding' test: user.is_active=false persisted. Test now auto-reactivates via motor at teardown, but if MONGO_URL differs in CI this teardown silently passes — recommend an admin endpoint POST /api/auth/reactivate or rely on seed_demo_data idempotency on restart.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase1a_lifecycle.py (NEW — 19 test cases across profile, documents, onboarding, offboarding, module-gate, tenant-isolation)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical paths (HR onboarding detail + offboarding detail + employee profile + employee self-service /app/me)

test_credentials: Used admin@hrms.io/Admin@123, hr@acme.io/Hr@12345, employee@acme.io/Employee@123, reseller@demo.io/Reseller@123. All valid per /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Tests created: (a) one additional onboarding instance for employee@acme.io (DOJ 2026-02-01) — auto-completed by test. (b) one offboarding instance for employee@acme.io — relieved at test-end. (c) TEST_Tenant_* throw-away company + admin user for cross-tenant check. Teardown reactivates employee@acme.io user/employee so subsequent iterations can log in. Leftovers: onboardings & offboardings history rows remain (acceptable audit trail).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Four pytest suites now guard HRMS: test_hrms_backend.py (25 core), test_workflows.py (19), test_modules_phase0.py (17), test_phase1a_lifecycle.py (19). Full run: pytest backend/tests/ -v (~2 min). Phase 1A: profile/documents/onboarding/offboarding routes mounted in server.py lines 78-81. Tenant isolation via TenantDB + middleware; module gating via requires_module('onboarding'). Note: a previously-terminated employee account is reactivated at end of TestOffboarding.test_complete_when_all_cleared_flips_terminated; if the testing agent ever sees 'Account is disabled' for employee@acme.io they should directly flip is_active=true in mongo users collection. On the frontend, /app/me uses /api/profile/me and determines edit rights from the backend `editable` map — FE should not hard-code role->section mapping.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. One non-issue during FE test: my Playwright selector for counting clearance items used `count('-')==1` to strip out 'clearance-status-*' testids but UUIDs already contain dashes, so the count read 0. Visual screenshot confirms all 8 clearance items render correctly (IT/admin/finance/hr/manager/security). No product defect.
